texto: The two of us are lIke sunshine and the rain. Together... We are the wellspring of all life. But between us, there will will bloom no flowers. We shall bear no fruit. For us, all of time... Shall be evergreen."And that's fine. I will be here always. To you... I give eternity."Hotaka
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Yuyuko Takemiya: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “The two of us are lIke sunshine and the rain. Together... We are the wellspring of all life. But …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, bittersweet, ya pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e ya.

frase_original: The two of us are lIke sunshine and the rain. Together... We are the wellspring of all life. But between us, there will will bloom no flowers. We shall bear no fruit. For us, all of time... Shall be evergreen."And that's fine. I will be here always. To you... I give eternity."Hotaka

texto: There is something in this world which no one has ever seen.It is soft and sweet.If it is spotted, I'm sure everyone will want to have it,Which is why no one has ever seen it.For this world has hidden it quite well, so that it is difficult to obtain.But, there will come a day when it is discovered by somebody,And only those who should obtain it will be able to find it.That is all.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Yuyuko Takemiya sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “There is something in this world which no one has ever seen.It is soft and sweet.If it is spotted…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, mystery, toradora, útil em momentos de toradora.
